data.current_description: Returns Jewish holidays, Torah readings, candle lighting times and other calendar events.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve Jewish calendar events and holidays
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves Jewish calendar events for a specified date range and location, including holidays, Torah readings, candle lighting times, and other observances. Accepts parameters for date range, geographic location, and event filtering. Returns a CalendarResponse object containing an array of CalendarEvent objects with event details, dates, and categories.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical input context (date range, location parameters) and output structure details. With 19 parameters, AI agents need clarity on what inputs are expected and how they affect results. Enhanced description explains the filtering capabilities and response structure to improve agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'getShabbatTimes' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description needs enhancement to specify required location inputs and clarify what times are returned. AI agents need to understand the geographic dependency and the specific time values provided to determine if this operation meets their needs.

data.current_summary: Get Shabbat candle lighting and havdalah times
data.current_description: Returns Shabbat times for a specific location.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves Shabbat candle lighting and havdalah times for a specified location and date. Requires location parameters (latitude/longitude or city) and optional date specification. Returns a response containing precise times for candle lighting (typically 18-40 minutes before sunset) and havdalah (after nightfall), along with related Shabbat observance times.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description needs enhancement to specify required location inputs and clarify what times are returned. AI agents need to understand the geographic dependency and the specific time values provided to determine if this operation meets their needs.

Description lacks specificity about input format (which calendar to provide) and output structure. data.current_summary: Convert between Hebrew and Gregorian dates
data.current_description: Converts dates between the Hebrew and Gregorian calendars.
data.description_suggestion: Converts dates between the Hebrew (Jewish) and Gregorian calendars. Accepts either a Hebrew date (year, month, day) or Gregorian date (year, month, day) as input and returns the equivalent date in the other calendar system. Returns a ConverterResponse object containing both calendar representations, day of week, and additional date metadata.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks specificity about input format (which calendar to provide) and output structure. Enhanced description clarifies the bidirectional nature of the conversion, specifies input parameters, and describes the response format to help AI agents understand how to construct requests and interpret results.

data.current_description: Returns 20-year projection of yahrzeit (memorial) or Hebrew anniversary dates.
data.description_suggestion: Calculates yahrzeit (Hebrew memorial date) or Hebrew anniversary dates for a given Hebrew date. Accepts a Hebrew date (year, month, day) and type parameter (yahrzeit or anniversary) as input. Returns a YahrzeitResponse containing a 20-year projection of corresponding dates in both Hebrew and Gregorian calendars, enabling users to identify when observances occur.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and uses appropriate domain terminology. Description mentions the 20-year projection but lacks critical input details (what Hebrew date to provide, how to specify yahrzeit vs. anniversary) and output structure. Enhanced description clarifies required inputs and response format for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Returns Torah reading (leyning/kriyah) details for Shabbat and holidays.
